name: Team Kasich
subject: Loose talk about war with North Korea is irresponsible
body:
We wanted to share with you Gov. John Kasich's latest Washington
Post op-ed. If you agree with his message, please consider
sharing this online.

Peter, we wanted to share with
you Gov. John Kasich's latest Washington Post op-ed.

If you agree with his message,
please consider sharing this online:

Loose talk about war with North
Korea is irresponsible
By: Gov. John Kasich

"With tensions continuing to
build between the United States and North Korea, there's growing
talk by politicians and TV pundits that we are on the brink of
war. In truth, we shouldn't be anywhere close.

This increasingly hot war of
words - including loose talk about the probability of war - does
nothing to bring us closer to where we need to be on North Korea,
especially when military options short of war remain on the
table. In fact, with millions of lives at stake, waging a war of
words is a distraction from the serious task at hand. Any kind of
war - especially nuclear war - should not be an option until all
other options are exhausted. And, in the case of North Korea,
there are several roads not yet taken.

First, North Korea is not, as
some claim, "sanctioned-out." We are nowhere near to applying the
same type of restraints on North Korea that were successful in
bringing Iran to the negotiating table.

In fact, the breadth of sanctions
we have placed on North Korea to date are far less than what was
applied in earlier crises on Russia, Syria and Iran. While our
sanctions on North Korea have clearly escalated, we still have
the option to penalize and seize the assets of North Korea's
enablers in other countries that enable Pyongyang to evade the
full brunt of financial measures. We can expand our focus on
shipping and work with our allies to deny maritime insurance to
the vessels heading to or from North Korea. Last month, we
targeted sanctions on 20 such vessels. Many more North Korean
vessels are active and engaged in illicit activities beyond the
small number designated by the Treasury Department.

We also can do more to expose
those who use North Korean slave labor and to block any
remittances back to Kim Jong Un's regime.

Second, none of this will work
without more pressure to hold the reluctant Chinese government
accountable for the commitments it has made and to target more
Chinese entities that support the North Korean government. The
overwhelming majority of North Korea's trade - 90 percent - is
with or facilitated by China, and despite agreed-upon U.N.
sanctions, much of this economic activity continues. Actions
should include targeting a greater number of Chinese banks that
deal with North Korea, fining their U.S. subsidiaries and
freezing their U.S. assets.

This year, the international
banking transaction network, known as SWIFT, moved to prevent
North Korean banks from using the global messaging system to
facilitate international transactions, but that doesn't impact
Chinese banks that transact for the North Koreans. We should
consider expanding this ban to include Chinese banks with any
North Korean connections.

Finally, we need to ask: Where
are our allies on all of this? Instead of threatening a bilateral
war between the United States and the North Korea, we should be
working with allies - including South Korea and Japan - to
threaten increased multilateral pressure to choke the North
Korean regime. While economic sanctions have not proved to be
effective yet, they have not been fully exhausted and tested.
Part of the reason the previous administration succeeded in
bringing Iran to the table - regardless of the flaws of their
final deal - was due to internationally coordinated sanctions.
Thankfully, the U.N. Security Council has adopted three rounds of
such sanctions this year, including significant measures last
week.

With millions of lives hanging in
the balance, the last thing we need is to have politicians and
pundits predicting odds on the probability of war. It's neither
an accurate nor a helpful way to treat a complex international
challenge."

--
Take the next step!
Share on Facebook (here) and
Twitter (here)

Thank you for all that you're
doing to help!

-Team Kasich

P.S. If you'd like to chip in to
support Gov. Kasich's message, please consider making a generous
donation at: JohnKasich.com/Donate

for America, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's
committee.

name: The White House
subject: Who America stands with
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8a798f6ecfc6ea60d9bbcd1962968343d6d8e63eceaae873a7ccdd3235ddf4d5442b50456b3eac81235538d838d1f369a24e6061a1df315d

Who America stands with

In the final days of 2017, the biggest anti-government protests in Iran since 2009 broke out. Demonstrations have now spread to at least 20 cities across the country.

“Years of mismanagement, corruption, and foreign adventurism have eroded the Iranian people’s trust in their leaders,” White House Press Secretary Sarah Sanders said. “The Iranian regime spends its people’s wealth on spreading militancy and terror abroad, rather than ensuring prosperity at home.”

US Ambassador to the United Nations Nikki Haley delivered a clear message at the UN yesterday, warning the international body that it cannot afford to repeat the mistake it made in 2009 by staying quiet.

“We must not be silent. The people of Iran are crying out for freedom.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8a798f6ecfc6ea60ae2740cbd6dcb94d14bcf7da60fee0a878a023e49680495c40c57035b467a708f05d29d024038006e0a129c433bf75f8
See what Ambassador Haley told the UN.

The forgotten American idea

James Pinkerton—co-chair of the bipartisan RATE Coalition, which stands for “Reforming America’s Taxes Equitably”—weighed in on the Republican Tax Cuts Act over the weekend in an op-ed for The Hill.

Pinkerton’s thesis? In polarized times, it’s easy to let partisan instincts cloud what should be a bipartisan consensus. “It should not be forgotten that the idea of using lower tax rates to stimulate growth is not just a Republican idea—it’s an American idea,” he writes.

“The soundest way to raise the revenues in the long run is to cut the rates now.” Those words didn't come from President Donald J. Trump—or even President Ronald Reagan.

They are from Democratic President John F. Kennedy.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8a798f6ecfc6ea60ae2740cbd6dcb94d14bcf7da60fee0a878a023e49680495c40c57035b467a708f05d29d024038006e0a129c433bf75f8
Read Pinkerton’s bipartisan case for tax cuts.

Looking back on year one of #MAGA

As President Trump and his Administration dive into a long list of priorities for 2018, it’s worth looking back on how far America has come in just one year.

- $5.5 trillion in tax cuts, 60 percent of which go to families
- The lowest unemployment rate in nearly 17 years
- 22 regulations eliminated for each new one enacted
- A record-setting 12 conservative federal circuit judges confirmed

And that list barely scratches the surface.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8a798f6ecfc6ea60938bd23fccf269dfe47b8d53836bbc833cdb31de3b9e4b4b31ae12286a65ed6b63282abaf00460361ca69462b7c46b17
See the full year one breakdown here.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump, Vice President Mike Pence, and Republican Legislators celebrate the passage of the Tax Cuts Act | December 20, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump will have lunch with Vice President Mike Pence, Secretary of State Rex Tillerson, and Secretary of Defense James Mattis.

The Vice President will participate in a swearing-in ceremony for Alabama’s Senator-elect Doug Jones and Minnesota’s Senator-designate Tina Smith at the U.S. Capitol.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8a798f6ecfc6ea606ecb6964069fe7f412144a87b8e0a32e26890a618f40641cfcb18c9654e5e73ba5177e15c7f66d5703e58bccfc10555b
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8a798f6ecfc6ea6049527bdb3dafc9378286533e5295bbad2519151d29744c39de696e04df006304ff68ccb23a213d4e1332b89f5678f6ba
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4862b6ad55d8052095165f3537da9957287556e5c6b5d62e59f1cfc8dba53e83d70962838d6a1cf82005cd075f250d72c
Unsubscribe

| It's 2018, and **[Congress of Counties](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e58349b779&e=1acd9bb3d6) is nearly here**! Registration is set to close no later than THIS Friday, January 5. [Reserve your spot](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=df47a9a2c7&e=1acd9bb3d6) **NOW** for our grassroots political training conference before our limited spots are gone.

Again, there are**limited spots remaining** and once they are gone, **registration will close for good**.
[**LAST CHANCE! REGISTER NOW**](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=86cbf0c98e&e=1acd9bb3d6)
With this [two-day political training conference](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=983e68e1f4&e=1acd9bb3d6) happening just **next week** (Friday, January 12 - Saturday, January 13), [register now before it's too late](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=80f1a9fe8d&e=1acd9bb3d6) to reserve your spot. **Friday is the _last day_ to register** \-- so make sure you book now before you miss out!

We'll cover**big topics** like**launching a campaign, campaign communications, fundraising, running a local party organization** and **MORE**.

Then we'll have a great time at a special Friday evening reception honoring and sponsored by our 2018 statewide team -- **Secretary of State Connie Lawson, Treasurer of State Kelly Mitchell and Auditor of State Tera Klutz** \-- with special guests **Governor Eric Holcomb** and **Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch**.

And then on Saturday afternoon, we'll**kickoff 2018** with the first election-year **joint appearance of our U.S. Senate candidates** and a **straw poll** showing where grassroots Hoosier Republicans stand _right now_ on this high-profile race.

[All of these Congress of Counties events](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=cff335dc4c&e=1acd9bb3d6) are available with a [full registration](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=217170834f&e=1acd9bb3d6) at $85/person. Or pick and choose which activities you'd like to attend [for just $25/event](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2cbfc643eb&e=1acd9bb3d6).

If you're a local Republican party officer, grassroots volunteer, candidate or future candidate,**this is an important event** that will help provide you with **critical skills you'll need** in 2018 and beyond!

Make sure you don't miss this important conference. [R](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3731424b53&e=1acd9bb3d6)[ _egister NOW_](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6646fe9af9&e=1acd9bb3d6) **before Friday's deadline and before we are fully booked!**

We have more than _**300 Hoosier Republicans**_ joining us for [Congress of Counties](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=51d3e7b707&e=1acd9bb3d6) next week -- will you be one of them?

subject: Stop GOP Cuts to Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security NOW
body:
Stop Republicans in Congress from gutting our earned benefit programs (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/No_Cuts_dems)

The petition to Congress reads:
"Do not cut, privatize, weaken or otherwise damage Medicare, Medicaid or Social Security. You cannot pay for your disastrous tax giveaway to Wall Street and to the top 1% with our earned benefits."

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/No_Cuts_dems)

Dear Activist,

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/No_Cuts_dems)

Donald Trump’s Republican lapdogs in Congress recently rammed a massively unpopular, cruel and deeply corrupt Tax Scam into law. Now, Paul Ryan and the rest of the right-wing Republican leadership in Congress is gearing up to slash Medicare, Medicaid, Social Security, nutrition assistance and more.1

Republican ideologues like Ryan know their Robin-Hood-in-reverse agenda to enrich their billionaire donors from Wall Street by gutting our earned benefit programs is deeply unpopular. They learned the hard way this past year how unpopular cutting Medicaid is. That is why they are predictably using made-up excuses about the deficit to smear these important programs.

We need to make it clear that any attempt to privatize, cut, weaken or damage Medicare, Medicaid, Social Security or any of our earned benefits is completely unacceptable.

Tell Congress: Stop the Republican attack on Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/No_Cuts_dems)

Even before the Trump Tax Scam passed, Ryan was talking about his plans to gut Medicaid and Medicare in 2018. “We're going to have to get back next year at entitlement reform,” he promised last month, suddenly rediscovering a passion for the deficit after pushing trillions in tax giveaways to the ultra-wealthy.2

Medicare is a fundamental tool of economic security for more than 58 million seniors, and the program is overwhelmingly popular.3,4 And throughout last summer, activists with the disability rights organization ADAPT and others showed Republicans just how important Medicaid is in their daily lives. The majority of Medicaid beneficiaries are children and working-age adults, Medicaid spends more money on long term care for seniors and people with disabilities than anything else.5 Social Security, meanwhile, is totally funded for the next two decades – longer if everyone pays at the same rate – and the only retirement income that most Americans can count on.6

Donald Trump repeatedly pledged during the 2016 campaign not to attack Medicare, Medicaid or our Social Security system but he has since seriously undermined these programs. Ryan’s ultimate goal is to end Medicare as we know it and replace it with a privatized program.7 Now, Trump and his lapdogs in Congress are not just coming after Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security, they are attacking programs like Meals on Wheels, SNAP, and other nutritional assistance that families rely on. Republicans are whipping up a fake deficit hysteria after giving massive giveaways to the rich as a Trojan horse to attack our earned benefits. We must go all out to resist any cuts.

Tell Congress: Stop the Republican attack on Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/No_Cuts_dems)

If Ryan gets his way, folks who have been working their whole lives counting on Medicare in their retirement won’t ever be able to enroll – instead they will get a check that might help cover their premium for private insurance, if they are lucky.8 And if the Republicans are successful in their attacks on Medicare and Medicaid, they will come next for Social Security.

What’s more, our Social Security system is separate from regular taxes and not affected by the deficit.9

Republicans cannot attack our earned benefits without a major fight. More than 70 percent of Americans rely on these programs at some point in our lives, and we are not going to let Ryan, McConnell and Trump dismantle them.10

Tell Congress: Stop the Republican attack on Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security. Click the link below to sign the petition:

https://act.credoaction.com/sign/No_Cuts_dems (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/No_Cuts_dems)

Thank you for your activism,

Kaili Lambe, Director of Organizing
CREDO Action from Working Assets (http://www.credoaction.com)

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/No_Cuts_dems) (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/No_Cuts_dems) (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/No_Cuts_dems)
Jeff Stein, “Ryan says Republicans to target welfare, Medicare, Medicaid spending in 2018 (https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/wonk/wp/2017/12/01/gop-eyes-post-tax-cut-changes-to-welfare-medicare-and-social-security/?utm_term=.374060865a2d) ,” The Washington Post, Dec. 6, 2017. Ibid. Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, “Medicare Enrollment Dashboard (https://www.cms.gov/Research-Statistics-Data-and-Systems/Statistics-Trends-and-Reports/Dashboard/Medicare-Enrollment/Enrollment%20Dashboard.html) ,” accessed Dec. 14, 2017. Dan Mangan, “Medicare, Medicaid popularity high: Kaiser (https://www.cnbc.com/2015/07/16/medicare-medicaid-popularity-high-ahead-of-birthday.html) ,” CNBC, July 17, 2017. Social Security Works, “Why Medicaid Is Important to Seniors (https://www.socialsecurityworks.org/wp-content/uploads/2017/03/Why-Medicaid-Is-Important-to-Seniors_FINAL.pdf) ,” accessed Dec. 20, 2017. Carolyn Colvin, "Social Security Funded Until 2034, and About Three-Quarters Funded for the Long Term; Many Options to Address the Long-Term Shortfall (https://blog.ssa.gov/social-security-funded-until-2034-and-about-three-quarters-funded-for-the-long-term-many-options-to-address-the-long-term-shortfall/) ," Social Security Matters, June 22, 2016. Josh Marshall, “Ryan Plans to Phase Out Medicare in 2017 (http://talkingpointsmemo.com/edblog/ryan-plans-to-phase-out-medicare-in-2017) ,” TPM, Nov. 13, 2016. clean_body: Stop Republicans in Congress from gutting our earned benefit programs () The petition to Congress reads: "Do not cut, privatize, weaken or otherwise damage Medicare, Medicaid or Social Security. You cannot pay for your disastrous tax giveaway to Wall Street and to the top 1% with our earned benefits." Add your name: () Dear Activist, () Donald Trump’s Republican lapdogs in Congress recently rammed a massively unpopular, cruel and deeply corrupt Tax Scam into law. Now, Paul Ryan and the rest of the right-wing Republican leadership in Congress is gearing up to slash Medicare, Medicaid, Social Security, nutrition assistance and more.1 Republican ideologues like Ryan know their Robin-Hood-in-reverse agenda to enrich their billionaire donors from Wall Street by gutting our earned benefit programs is deeply unpopular. They learned the hard way this past year how unpopular cutting Medicaid is. That is why they are predictably using made-up excuses about the deficit to smear these important programs. We need to make it clear that any attempt to privatize, cut, weaken or damage Medicare, Medicaid, Social Security or any of our earned benefits is completely unacceptable. Tell Congress: Stop the Republican attack on Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security. Click here to sign the petition. () Even before the Trump Tax Scam passed, Ryan was talking about his plans to gut Medicaid and Medicare in 2018. “We're going to have to get back next year at entitlement reform,” he promised last month, suddenly rediscovering a passion for the deficit after pushing trillions in tax giveaways to the ultra-wealthy.2 Medicare is a fundamental tool of economic security for more than 58 million seniors, and the program is overwhelmingly popular.3,4 And throughout last summer, activists with the disability rights organization ADAPT and others showed Republicans just how important Medicaid is in their daily lives. The majority of Medicaid beneficiaries are children and working-age adults, Medicaid spends more money on long term care for seniors and people with disabilities than anything else.5 Social Security, meanwhile, is totally funded for the next two decades – longer if everyone pays at the same rate – and the only retirement income that most Americans can count on.6 Donald Trump repeatedly pledged during the 2016 campaign not to attack Medicare, Medicaid or our Social Security system but he has since seriously undermined these programs. Ryan’s ultimate goal is to end Medicare as we know it and replace it with a privatized program.7 Now, Trump and his lapdogs in Congress are not just coming after Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security, they are attacking programs like Meals on Wheels, SNAP, and other nutritional assistance that families rely on. Republicans are whipping up a fake deficit hysteria after giving massive giveaways to the rich as a Trojan horse to attack our earned benefits. We must go all out to resist any cuts. Tell Congress: Stop the Republican attack on Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security. Click here to sign the petition. () If Ryan gets his way, folks who have been working their whole lives counting on Medicare in their retirement won’t ever be able to enroll – instead they will get a check that might help cover their premium for private insurance, if they are lucky.8 And if the Republicans are successful in their attacks on Medicare and Medicaid, they will come next for Social Security. What’s more, our Social Security system is separate from regular taxes and not affected by the deficit.9 Republicans cannot attack our earned benefits without a major fight. More than 70 percent of Americans rely on these programs at some point in our lives, and we are not going to let Ryan, McConnell and Trump dismantle them.10 Tell Congress: Stop the Republican attack on Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security. **For Immediate Release:** 1/3/2018
**Contact:** Patrick Hynes, (202) 321-5310
CONCORD - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement following the resignation of Democrat Representative Thomas Katsiantonis:
"The Democrats in Concord lost one of their unhinged members today as Representative Thomas Katsiantonis finally resigned under a cloud of a federal tax evasion investigation. Too bad he’s the only one. Unhinged Rep. Sherry Frost – who has homicidal fantasies about her state representative colleagues – and Rep. Kathy Rogers – who pleaded guilty to assaulting an election volunteer still terrorize the house as Democrat ‘leaders’ like Steve Shurtleff remain silent. New Hampshire Democrats are out of touch and out of control.”******BACKGROUND**

subject: We took another look at the map
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c69e1/24c69fd9/140a02f6e/1893163e/829995064/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEM02N_digital_email_20180103_598NewYear_inact&source=WQEFEM02N_digital_email_20180103_598NewYear_inact")
---

Peter,

Happy New Year!

I hope you're ready to hit the ground running for 2018, because we've set some ambitious goals for the next 11 months -- including recruiting women to run for 598 critical state legislative seats.

It's a huge undertaking, our biggest effort to remake the face of state politics ever. But I know this community is up to the challenge.

**[Donate $3 to help recruit, train, and build winning campaigns for women who will run for the 598 seats we're targeting.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c69e1/24c69fd9/140a02f6e/1893163f/829995064/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180102_email_598NewYear?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+recruit+train+and+build&utm_campaign=WQEFEM02N_digital_email_20180103_598NewYear_inact&source=WQEFEM02N_digital_email_20180103_598NewYear_inact")**

Our political landscape has changed _so much_ since the beginning of 2017.

That's because of our history-making wins in state and local races over the past year -- we turned several seats in areas Trump won blue and helped 11 women flip seats in the Virginia House of Delegates.

Those victories inspired us to go back to the map and look at which other seats we could target, and we landed on a whopping 598.

Electing pro-choice Democratic women to those seats will fundamentally alter the makeup of state legislatures -- breaking up Republican supermajorities in some and entirely flipping others from red to blue.

And how we spend these next 11 months will be crucial to making this a reality.

**Donate $3 to make sure we have the resources to recruit, train, and elect hundreds of pro-choice Democratic women to state legislatures this year:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Buckle-Down](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c69e1/24c69fd9/140a02f6e/18931638/829995064/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180102_email_598NewYear?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgBuckleDown&utm_campaign=WQEFEM02N_digital_email_20180103_598NewYear_inact&source=WQEFEM02N_digital_email_20180103_598NewYear_inact")**

Thanks,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: Thank you for being a critical part of this fight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
As we prepare ourselves for the critical task ahead in 2018, I want to thank you for your unwavering support and activism throughout the past year.

One year ago we faced the daunting reality of a Trump presidency and a solidly Republican Congress. The challenges were enormous, but grassroots Democrats like you did not shirk from the fight. You stood firm and fought hard to defend our values.

Thanks to your support and engagement, we were able to energize Americans across this country and realize important accomplishments -- including holding back the GOP's efforts to totally repeal the Affordable Care Act, and winning strong electoral victories this past fall such as the historic election of the first Democratic Senator in Alabama since 1992.

**I continue to be inspired by the commitment and dedication of supporters like you -- and I know that if you continue to stay engaged, we can take our country back in 2018.**

Thank you again for being a critical part of this fight.

I look forward to working together to make this new year a year when we translate purposeful action into hard-fought progress.

Chris Van Hollen

U.S. Senator

---

Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
---
Van Hollen for Senate
10605 Concord Street, Suite 202
Kensington, MD 20895

Today, the Republicans opened the Senate up again for business.

And they're returning to Washington with big plans that will have horrible consequences for seniors and working families.

Before the break, Paul Ryan said his next top priority is "entitlement reform" \-- code for massive cuts to Social Security and Medicare. Republicans are also planning new attacks on Obamacare. And they still haven't provided the Children's Health Insurance Program with long-term funding.

Democratic Senators are going to have to fight hard to protect the progress we made under President Obama from these new attacks -- and they need your support.
